rounds.agent_message: Seed round copied from the highest existing source round.

Implemented the round output in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p55/optimization_rounds/round1/output_model_new.py), keeping the MFMA path explicit with `S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32`, fixed `128 x 128` block tiling, the required lane/fragment mapping, and the specified accumulator unpack/writeback mapping. The module now also caches the device weight tensor by storage pointer via `_get_cached_weight`, so `forward()` does not rebuild cached device state unless the underlying weight storage changes. I also updated the round path in [test_conv2d_asym.py](/workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py) and [bench_conv2d.py](/workspace/substrate/benchmark/conv2d/bench_conv2d.py), and wrote [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p55/optimization_rounds/round1/case.txt) with the required round-directory path. Validation: `python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py` passed with `2 passed`.

Implemented the split-K rewrite in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p55/optimization_rounds/round2/output_model_new.py): the kernel now launches `SPLIT_K_SLICES = 2` partial MFMA tiles, reduces partial fp32 sums with `S.amdgpu.buffer_atomic_add_f32` into a cached fp32 workspace, and runs a separate store kernel to remap the workspace back to NCHW output. The forward path also caches weight/workspace/output tensors so steady-state calls reuse device allocations. Wrote the required marker file at [case.txt](/workspace/kernel_benchmark/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p55/optimization_rounds/round2/path/to/case.txt). Validation: - `python -m pytest /workspace/substrate/test/examples/conv2d/amdgpu/test_conv2d_asym.py` passed: `2 passed in 1.94s` - Direct round2 MLIR sanity check passed: the split kernel emits both MFMA and atomic-fadd - Direct round2 execution is still blocked by a substrate lowering failure in MFMA codegen; the same lowering failure reproduces on the provided starting kernel, so I could not obtain a successful numeric runtime check in this workspace
